creator: ohauer@FreeBSD.org
text:
Additional Note that I already had forgotten.

The pkg-install script tests if there is a tty, if not the pkg-install script does not ask but installs a copy of the mailer.conf as /usr/local/share/postfix/mailer.conf.postfix. Unluckily the output with the hint is not displayed by salt.

# salt pftest\* pkg.install postfix
svnconv.abaxx.net:
    ----------
    postfix:
        ----------
        new:
            3.1.1,1
        old:
-------------------------------------------
Summary
-------------------------------------------
# of minions targeted: 1
# of minions returned: 1
# of minions that did not return: 0
-------------------------------------------

Since 2016-02 the pkg-install script supports installation without tty, shortened output via ssh install (also no tty)
ssh pftest 'pkg install -y postfix'
...
New packages to be INSTALLED:
        postfix: 3.1.1,1
...

If postfix is *not* already activated in /usr/local/etc/mail/mailer.conf
  - mv /usr/local/etc/mail/mailer.conf /usr/local/etc/mail/mailer.conf.old
  - install -m 0644 /usr/local/share/postfix/mailer.conf.postfix /usr/local/etc/mail/mailer.conf


If the system is running FreeBSD < 10.3 the hint points to /etc/mail/ else $LOCALBASE/mail

Unluckily we have no option to pass anything else then "env vars" to the install script, therefore we can only test if tty is available and if not skip the question.

If you have an idea how to make the installer more salt friendly I'm open for discussion and improving the pkg-install script.
